prompt: Write an email for 'InviteJoy' — a digital invitation platform — to nudge users who are currently using a free template to upgrade to a premium template. The user has already picked a free template and started customizing it but hasn't published yet. Premium templates offer: animated elements, custom fonts, RSVP tracking, photo galleries, and matching thank-you cards. Premium upgrade costs $9.99 per invitation. The email should show what they're missing without making them feel bad about the free choice. Tone: encouraging, aspirational, not pushy. Target: global audience, primarily women 25-45 planning celebrations.

assertions.text: Has subject line and preview text
Shows premium benefits without shaming the free template choice
Mentions specific premium features (animations, RSVP, photo gallery, etc.)
Has a clear CTA to upgrade or preview premium templates
Tone is encouraging and aspirational, not guilt-tripping
